content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 31 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Base text:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 116v–116r (LC PDF pp. 229–230) | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pp. 162–163

---

**1** And Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel. **2** And he said to them: "I am a hundred and twenty years old today. I am no longer able to go out and come in, and the LORD has said to me: 'You shall not cross over this Jordan.' **3** The LORD your God — he is the one crossing over before you. He will destroy these nations before you, and you shall dispossess them. Joshua — he is the one crossing over before you, as the LORD has spoken. **4** And the LORD will do to them as he did to Sihon and Og, the kings of the Amorites, and to their land, when he destroyed them. **5** And the LORD will give them over to you, and you shall do to them according to all the commandment which I have commanded you. **6** Be strong and courageous [chizqu ve-imtsu]. Do not be afraid or be in dread of them, for the LORD your God — he is the one walking with you; he will not leave you and he will not forsake you."

**7** Then Moses summoned Joshua and said to him before the eyes of all Israel: "Be strong and courageous [chazaq ve-emats], for you are the one who will go with this people into the land which the LORD swore to their fathers to give to them, and you shall cause them to inherit it. **8** And the LORD — he is the one walking before you; he will be with you; he will not leave you and he will not forsake you. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed."

**9** Then Moses wrote this instruction [torah] and gave it to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and to all the elders of Israel. **10** And Moses commanded them, saying: "At the end of every seven years, at the appointed time in the year of release [shemittah], at the Feast of Booths [Sukkot], **11** when all Israel comes to appear before the LORD your God in the place which he will choose, you shall read this instruction [torah] before all Israel in their hearing. **12** Assemble the people — the men and the women and the little ones, and the sojourner who is within your towns — so that they may hear and learn, and they shall fear the LORD your God and they shall take care to do all the words of this instruction [torah]. **13** And their children, who have not known it, shall hear and learn to fear the LORD your God all the days that you live upon the land which you are crossing the Jordan to possess it."

**14** And the LORD said to Moses: "Behold, your days are drawing near to die. Call Joshua and present yourselves in the tent of meeting, that I may commission him." So Moses and Joshua went and presented themselves in the tent of meeting. **15** And the LORD appeared in the tent in a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood over the entrance of the tent.

**16** And the LORD said to Moses: "Behold, you are about to lie down with your fathers, and this people will rise up and go whoring after the foreign gods of the land into which they are going, and they will forsake me and break my covenant which I made with them. **17** And my anger will be kindled against them on that day, and I will forsake them and hide my face from them, and they will be devoured. And many evils and troubles will come upon them, so that they will say on that day: 'Have not these evils come upon us because our God is not among us?' **18** And I will surely hide my face on that day because of all the evil which they have done, for they have turned to other gods.

**19** "And now, write for yourselves this song and teach it to the sons of Israel; put it in their mouths, so that this song may be a witness for me against the sons of Israel. **20** For when I bring them into the land flowing with milk and honey, which I swore to their fathers, and they eat and are satisfied and grow fat, they will turn to other gods and serve them, and despise me and break my covenant. **21** And when many evils and troubles come upon them, this song shall confront them as a witness — for it will not be forgotten from the mouth of their offspring — for I know their inclination [yitser], which they are inclined to do even today, before I bring them into the land which I swore."

**22** So Moses wrote this song on that day and taught it to the sons of Israel.

**23** And he commissioned Joshua son of Nun and said: "Be strong and courageous [chazaq ve-emats], for you will bring the sons of Israel into the land which I swore to them, and I will be with you."

**24** And when Moses had finished writing the words of this instruction [torah] in a scroll to the very end, **25** Moses commanded the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD, saying: **26** "Take this scroll of the instruction [sefer ha-torah] and place it at the side of the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and it shall be there as a witness against you. **27** For I know your rebellion and your stiff neck. Behold, while I am still alive with you today, you have been rebellious against the LORD — and how much more after my death! **28** Assemble to me all the elders of your tribes and your officials, and I will speak these words in their hearing and call heaven and earth to witness against them. **29** For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly and turn from the way which I commanded you; and evil will befall you in the latter days, for you will do evil in the eyes of the LORD, provoking him to anger through the work of your hands."

**30** And Moses spoke in the hearing of all the assembly of Israel the words of this song, until their completion:

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**V.1 — MT vs. LXX opening:** The Leningrad Codex reads וַיֵּלֶךְ מֹשֶׁה (vayelech Moshe, "And Moses went"), while the LXX Vaticanus reads "And Moses finished speaking all these words" — a different Hebrew Vorlage. All three agents follow the MT. SOPHIA adopts the MT reading. The LXX variant is noted for reference; PNEUMA slightly smoothed this by rendering "Moses finished speaking" without textual justification — see disagreements.

**V.6 — "go out and come in":** The Hebrew idiom lo-ukhal od latset vavo refers to active leadership capacity, not mere mobility (cf. Num 27:17). The rendering "go out and come in" faithfully preserves the idiom while English readers understand its import. The double promise "he will not leave you and he will not forsake you" is rendered consistently both times it appears (vv. 6 and 8).

**"Be strong and courageous":** The Hebrew chazaq ve-emats appears three times (vv. 6, 7, 23), forming a threefold commissioning: to all Israel, to Joshua before all Israel, and then from the LORD to Joshua directly. The bracketed Hebrew is retained in the consensus to preserve the formulaic character.

**V.10 — Hakhel institution:** LOGOS adds the Hebrew gloss [shemittah] at "year of release" and [Sukkot] at "Feast of Booths." SOPHIA adopts both glosses as they clarify the specific institutions without paraphrasing.

**V.16 — "go whoring":** The Hebrew zanah (whoring/prostituting oneself after) is the standard biblical idiom for apostasy. EMET preserves "go whoring" (literal), LOGOS renders "whore after," PNEUMA "prostitute themselves." SOPHIA adopts "go whoring" as the most established rendering that preserves the covenantal-betrayal metaphor without euphemism.

**V.21 — yitser:** The rare anthropological term יִצְרוֹ (yitser, "inclination/formed-impulse") is rendered "inclination" following EMET. This is the only occurrence in Deuteronomy and has significant resonance with later rabbinic yetser ha-ra theology. LOGOS's "what they are inclined to do" paraphrases rather than translates the noun. SOPHIA renders "their inclination [yitser]" with the bracketed gloss.

**V.26 — "at the side of the ark":** מִצַּד אֲרוֹן (mi-tsad aron) — beside the ark, not within it. Both the MT and LXX confirm this placement. The scroll sits alongside the tablets rather than replacing them.

— *SOPHIA*
